issues.description: Prior issue: outline credited Leibniz for dy/dx and Lagrange for f'(x), which were unsupported by the reference. Revision removes both name attributions and explicitly states 'Refer to the two main forms simply as the dy/dx form and the f'(x) form — no name attributions.' Narration_seed also no longer mentions Leibniz or Lagrange.

Prior issue: trap #3 was framed narrowly as 'forgetting the negative sign in d/dx[cos x] and in cot/csc derivatives,' which was more specific than the reference's 'Misapplying product/quotient rule with wrong sign conventions.' Revision now reads 'Wrong sign conventions in trig derivatives — forgetting the minus on d/dx[cos x] and on the cotangent/cosecant derivatives,' which adopts the reference's 'wrong sign conventions' phrasing while keeping the pedagogically useful trig specificity. Also note: the existing trap #1 (quotient rule numerator sign/order error) directly covers the reference's product/quotient sign-convention trap, so the trap list as a whole now fully aligns with the reference.

External resources 'OpenStax Calculus Vol 1 Ch 3.1–3.6' and 'Khan Academy AP Calculus AB — Derivatives 1' remain. These are not listed in the reference document, but as noted in the prior review, they are plausible real resources. No change required if independently verified.
issues.fix: Optional — verify titles/chapter numbers against actual OpenStax and Khan Academy catalogs.

overall_comment: Both flagged issues from the prior review are cleanly resolved. Leibniz/Lagrange attributions removed in 06_notation (slide and narration). Trap #3 reframed to use the reference's 'wrong sign conventions' phrasing while preserving the trig-derivative specificity. All 10 CB topic codes (CHA 2.1–2.3, FUN 2.4–2.10) and the module title continue to match the reference exactly. No new unsupported attributions or claims introduced in the revision. The OpenStax/Khan Academy resource names in section 18 remain unverified by the reference but are plausible — same status as before, not a regression. Verdict: pass.
